items.title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Restart of US-Iran Peace Talks
items.summary: Gold fell as efforts to resume peace talks between the US and Iran remained at an impasse, two months into a war that’s upended global markets and raised inflation risks.

items.title: Nothing Phone (4a) Review: Stylish design and strong performance but... Worth buying?
items.summary: Nothing Phone (4a) Review: Nothing recently launched its mid-range smartphone in India – the Nothing Phone (4a) – at a starting price of Rs 31,999. Let's find out all about it in this detailed review.

items.title: Oil advances with Middle East in focus, equities rise slightly
items.summary: GLOBAL-MARKETS/ (WRAPUP 7):GLOBAL MARKETS-Oil advances with Middle East in focus, equities rise slightly

items.title: A Warsh-Led Fed Stands to Reignite the Treasuries Steepener Bet
items.summary: Wall Street strategists say that a Kevin Warsh-led Federal Reserve may jolt the $31 trillion Treasuries market out of its narrow trading ranges, urging investors to position for shorter-dated yields to eventually move lower.

items.title: US stocks: S&P 500, Nasdaq, close slightly up in cautious start to a heavy earnings week
items.summary: U.S. stocks saw modest gains as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq eked out small increases in muted trading. Investors are digesting recent record highs and anticipating a busy week with earnings reports, economic data, and the Federal Reserve's rate decision. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East also remain a concern.

items.title: Carney unveils $18 billion Canadian government-owned investment fund
items.summary: Carney unveils $18 billion Canadian government-owned investment fund

items.title: IHH Healthcare seeks its investor Mitsui's help to set up talks with Daiichi in a ₹11,800 crore dispute
items.summary: Malaysia-based hospital operator IHH Healthcare has reached out to its largest shareholder Mitsui & Co. to help open communication with Japanese drugmaker Daiichi Sankyo, even as both sides continue legal battles in India and Japan over damages worth about ₹11,800 crore.

items.title: Organon gives Sun Pharma scale and reach, but challenge lies in integration, execution
items.summary: Sun Pharma has acquired Organon & Co for $11.75 billion. This deal provides Sun Pharma with expanded global scale and commercial reach. It also offers diversification into new segments like women's health and biosimilars. The acquisition is expected to strengthen Sun Pharma's position for future growth. Managing integration and debt will be key.

items.title: GCCs, large tech companies keep office leasing market robust
items.summary: India’s office market recorded its strongest-ever first quarter in January–March, with gross leasing rising 10% year-on-year to 21.5 million sq ft, driven largely by pre-committed deals from big occupiers.

items.title: RBI finalises new risk weight norms
items.summary: To be eligible, loans must be extended to individuals or small businesses with turnover up to ?500 crore, fall under standard retail products, remain within a ?10 crore exposure cap per borrower, and form part of a diversified portfolio where no single borrower accounts for more than 0.2%.

items.title: 'Trade deals with US, EU soon': Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India is set to sign trade agreements with the European Union and the United States in the coming months. Following a pact with New Zealand, these deals signal India's expanding global trade reach. Additionally, discussions for a free trade agreement with the Russia-led Eurasian Economic Union are scheduled for June in Moscow.

items.title: Govt reviews divestment plans, IDBI Bank stake sale
items.summary: Government officials are reviewing the IDBI Bank disinvestment strategy. This comes after weak investor interest and market volatility impacted the proposed sale. A core group of secretaries is deliberating on revised valuations and timelines. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman confirmed the strategic sale will proceed. The government aims to conclude the IDBI Bank transaction soon.

items.title: Expected credit loss rules: New provisioning framework to come into force in April 2027
items.summary: The new ECL guidelines would introduce a "staging framework" for asset classification under the ECL approach, replacing the existing incurred-loss-based provisioning framework. However, the central bank said in a note that it would retain the current 90-day overdue rule for classifying non-performing assets (NPA).

items.title: Power demand surges to 255.8 GW on Monday
items.summary: India's power demand surged to 255.8 GW on Monday. This record was set due to intense heatwaves across the country. Delhi's power demand also crossed 7,000 MW for the first time in April. Tata Power Delhi Distribution met its highest demand of 2,030 MW this summer. The power ministry anticipates further increases in demand.

items.title: Leverage FTAs to widen market access: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with exporters and industry groups. They discussed ways to boost India's exports and utilize free trade agreements. Exporters requested extensions for export obligations and wider trade relief measures. Concerns about shipping costs and the need for an Indian shipping line were also raised. India's goods exports saw a decline in March.

items.title: Philips looks to step up play in India’s growing discretionary market
items.summary: Philips says global macroeconomic volatility has hit businesses over the past five years, but underlying demand trends are still supporting near-term growth, according to Deeptha Khanna, executive vice president and chief business leader (personal health).

items.title: India growth to soften in near term, rebound later: Nomura
items.summary: India's economy is set for a slowdown in the first half of FY27. Supply disruptions are expected to impact growth. However, demand remains strong. The economy is projected to recover in the latter half of the year. Nomura forecasts a full-year growth of 6.8%. Supportive policies and easing trade tensions are positive factors. The Iran conflict poses a potential risk.

items.title: CEA Anantha Nageswaran flags energy risk to growth story
items.summary: India faces energy market volatility impacting farmers and workers. Chief Economic Adviser V Anantha Nageswaran highlights dependence on imported fossil fuels. A NITI Aayog roadmap proposes Digital Public Infrastructure 2.0 and 3.0. This strategy aims for broad-based societal capability and inclusive growth. It focuses on transforming MSMEs, agriculture, education, and health.

items.title: Heatwave alert: Centre issues advisory, work hours may shift
items.summary: India's Union labour and health ministries are issuing urgent heat-wave advisories. States and union territories must ensure adequate rest and cooling for outdoor and factory workers. Measures to prevent and manage heat stroke cases are also directed. Dedicated heat stroke units will be set up in health facilities. Early warnings will be disseminated to communities.

items.title: Weaker monsoon prediction, Iran war cloud growth outlook
items.summary: India's rural economy faces potential challenges. A weaker monsoon and rising farm costs from the Iran war could impact growth this fiscal year. While strong water reserves and food stocks offer some buffer, rural demand may slow in the latter half of FY27. Economists are closely watching these developments for their effect on the economy.

items.title: SBI cards sells Rs 1,800-crore bad loans to Integro Finserv
items.summary: SBI Cards has taken a bold step in fortifying its financial health by offloading a considerable portion of its stressed credit card debt, valued at approximately ₹1,800 crore, to Integro Finserv. This transaction is a critical aspect of SBI Cards' plan to address mounting delinquencies and elevate the overall quality of its credit card offerings.

items.title: Do not foresee any requirement to import petrol or diesel: Petroleum Ministry
items.summary: Concerns over stock supplies amidst the West Asia conflict and potential increase in prices of petrol and diesel have led to panic-buying among consumers

items.title: Coal India Q4 net profit accelerates 12%, fuelled by marginal increase in input cost
items.summary: Cost of inputs rose by a marginal 1% in the March-end quarter

RBI declines pleas for more time, ECL to be effective from April 2027
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will implement a new Expected Credit Loss provisioning system from April 1, 2025. Banks must adopt this proactive approach, moving away from current loss-based provisioning. The RBI has provided transition measures to ease the shift. The central bank also confirmed that Non-Performing Asset classification will remain.

items.title: Mumbai-Solapur Vande Bharat Express derails in Pune; none hurt; train services hit
items.summary: A Vande Bharat Express coach derailed near Pune railway station on Monday evening. No passengers were injured in the incident. The derailment disrupted train services. Railway authorities are working to restore operations. The crossing where the incident occurred is scheduled for an upgrade. Several trains faced delays or cancellations due to the event.

items.title: DeFi Rescue Hurts Anti-Wall Street Pitch After $10 Billion Run
items.summary: Decentralized finance is in the midst of the largest coordinated rescue in its history, an effort marked by moral hazard concerns and ad-hoc coordination that sit awkwardly with the sector’s founding pitch as a disruptor of traditional finance.

items.title: Centre assures ample fertiliser stocks at onset of kharif season; rates steady
items.summary: Official data has shown that fertiliser stocks amounting to 49 per cent for the 2026 kharif season are available in the country, marking adequate levels of the agricultural input.

items.title: Samsung Electronics may withdraw home appliance and TV sales from China: Report
items.summary: The company could make a final decision as early as the end of April, marking a major shift in its China strategy.

items.title: Govt floats mega fertiliser tender, seeks 85 MT of nutrient imports to bolster reserves ahead of main planting season
items.summary: India has floated a global tender to import 50 million tonnes of NPK fertilisers and 35 million tonnes of ammonium sulphate to strengthen nutrient reserves ahead of the June sowing season.

items.title: India’s Russian oil imports cool 20% in April after March spike
items.summary: India’s imports of Russian crude fell 20% month-on-month in April to 1.57 million barrels per day, cooling after a March surge driven by floating cargoes during the Iran conflict and a temporary US sanctions waiver.

items.title: 8th Pay Commission: Who is Chairperson Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai, former Supreme Court judge?
items.summary: Born in Mumbai and having served as a judge of the Supreme Court of India, Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai is presently serving as the Chairperson of the 8th Central Pay Commission from 3 November 2025.

items.title: Nvidia doubles down on India: 7.6 lakh sq ft Bengaluru deal its single largest setup as chipmaker accelerates India plans
items.summary: Chipmaker Nvidia has secured a massive 7,60,000 sq ft office space in Bengaluru. This 10-year lease is India's largest office commitment to date. The deal significantly expands Nvidia's presence in India, now totaling over 11.35 lakh sq ft across six cities. This expansion fuels its global AI demand and leverages India's engineering talent.

items.title: Centre eyes Rs 5,000 crore relief, China’s Hotan route to ease airline stress amid Iran war: Report
items.summary: The Indian government is considering multiple relief measures for airlines facing high operating costs and reduced traffic due to the ongoing US-Iran conflict. A Rs 5,000 crore emergency credit line guarantee scheme is being fast-tracked, with individual airline limits of Rs 1,500 crore.

items.title: India never offers any duty concessions in dairy sector under FTAs: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India has not offered any tariff concessions in the dairy sector under any free trade agreement so far, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Monday, including pacts with the European Union, the UK, Australia and New Zealand.

items.title: India–New Zealand sign 'once-in-a-generation' FTA
items.summary: India and New Zealand signed a “once-in-a-generation” free trade agreement on Monday that gives New Delhi duty-free access to 100% of tariff lines, aimed at boosting exports from sectors such as textiles, leather, pharmaceuticals and processed foods.

items.title: China vows countermeasures if EU enacts 'Made in Europe' plan
items.summary: The EU unveiled in March new "Made in Europe" rules for companies trying to access public funds in strategic sectors including cars, green tech and steel, obliging firms to meet minimum thresholds for EU-made parts

The Ministry of External Affairs said the issue is complicated by the current regional conflict, with officials noting that India remains engaged with all concerned parties as the situation evolves.

Both properties are gems in India's commercial landscape, with EcoWorld 21 perched on Bengaluru's Outer Ring Road and RMZ Edge in the vibrant Koregaon Park of Pune.

No Petrol: In a move aimed at curbing rising air pollution, the Delhi Government has made it mandatory for vehicles to carry a valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate to access fuel. Vehicles without a valid PUC will not be supplied petrol, diesel or CNG at fuel stations in the national capital.

items.summary: Shares of Borosil Glass Works have increased threefold over the past one year as the company has posted strong revenue and EBIDTA growth over the past few quarters.

items.title: Why you should not be always chasing returns
items.summary: Most people focus on returns and chase products that offer the best returns at any point.

items.title: Jhunjhunwala-partnered fund buys stake in TFCI: What's buzzing?
items.summary: In an interview with CNBC-TV18, Satpal Arora, MD of Tourism Finance Corporation of India (TFCI), talked about the state of business and the outlook ahead.

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.title: Tech up to a healthier world
items.summary: Sensors, 3D printed skin, wearables, reconstructed organs, revolutionized clinical trials, personalized medicine and what not – get ready for a new era of healthcare

items.title: Steel stocks rally as govt extends MIP on 66 steel products
items.summary: Stocks like Hindustan Zinc, Hindalco, Jindal Steel, JSW Steel and Tata Steel gained over 1 percent from previous close.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. It already raised Rs 348.52 crore through anchor investors' portion on Tuesday, the day before issue opening.

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
